channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenians vote in pivotal election with Russia-West tensions in focus
item.description: Prime Minister Pashinyan's pro-Western ruling party faces opposition candidates favoring closer ties with Russia, in a vote that could reshape the South Caucasus country's geopolitical alignment.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo XIV draws 1.2 million to Madrid Mass, challenges Europe on Christian roots
item.description: Over a million people filled Madrid's streets for Pope Leo's outdoor Mass, where he urged Europe to acknowledge Christianity's contributions to its cultural identity and said God stands with the poor.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South Africa's Ramaphosa vows crackdown on xenophobic violence amid migration tensions
item.description: President Ramaphosa announced tougher immigration enforcement and penalties for employers hiring undocumented workers, while distancing the government from vigilante immigration checks.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's negotiator threatens US bases and Israeli assets over Lebanon escalation
item.description: Iran's top negotiator declared US bases and Israeli assets in the Middle East "legitimate targets" following Israel's strikes on Beirut and the US naval blockade of Iran.

item.title: ☢️ US pushes IAEA resolution demanding Iran account for bombed nuclear sites
item.description: The US is lobbying countries on the IAEA Board to back a resolution requiring Iran to disclose what happened to its bombed nuclear facilities and enriched uranium stocks.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Coast Guard Expels Chinese Ships from Restricted Waters
item.description: Taiwan's coast guard expelled four Chinese government vessels that entered restricted waters off the island's south, with both sides exchanging warnings in a rare escalation of tensions across the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan-Administered Kashmir Court Clears Path for Refugee Elections
item.description: The top court in Pakistan-administered Kashmir upheld 12 legislative seats reserved for Kashmiri refugees, clearing the way for upcoming elections in the disputed territory.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ IDF reveals major Hezbollah command tunnel network in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military published footage of a Hezbollah tunnel system near Beaufort Castle, showing sophisticated infrastructure built with Iranian support for coordinating attacks.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇮🇱 IRGC-linked media says Iran will respond to Israeli strikes on Beirut
item.description: A source close to Irans Revolutionary Guard told Saberin news outlet that Iran will respond to Israels violation of the ceasefire following airstrikes in southern Beirut.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇫 US sending third-country deportees to Central African Republic
item.description: The Trump administration has arranged for the impoverished Central African Republic to accept deportees from third countries amid widespread protests against US immigration policies.

item.title: 🇨🇳🌧️ China activates flood emergency response in six regions amid heavy rain forecast
item.description: Chinese authorities warned of rising river levels, flash floods and mountain torrents as rainstorms are expected to continue through Tuesday across six provinces.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel Strikes Beirut Suburbs Days After US-Brokered Ceasefire
item.description: Israel carried out strikes on Beiruts southern suburbs without warning, targeting Hezbollah infrastructure just days after a US-supported ceasefire plan was announced. The strikes risk escalating tensions as Hezbollah had rejected the ceasefire terms.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Agrees Fourth Oil Output Hike Despite Iran War Disruptions
item.description: OPEC+ approved a fourth consecutive monthly increase in oil output targets, raising July production by 188,000 barrels per day. The move comes despite the ongoing US-Iran conflict preventing several members from pumping more.

item.title: ⚽ Iran World Cup Team Lands in Mexico Amid US Visa Dispute
item.description: Irans soccer team arrived in Tijuana, Mexico for the World Cup as Tehran accused Washington of obstructing team preparations. The diplomatic row highlights tensions between the two nations even as they negotiate an end to the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇯🇵🇵🇭 Beijing sends largest patrol ship east of Taiwan after Japan-Philippine boundary talks
item.description: China deployed its largest patrol vessel near Taiwan following Japan-Philippine maritime boundary discussions. Communist Party mouthpiece warned the two countries are becoming a "source of trouble" and putting regional peace at risk.

item.title: 🇫🇮🇪🇺🇷🇺 Finland's Stubb urges Europe to step up and lead peace talks with Russia
item.description: Finnish President Alexander Stubb called for Europe to take a more active role in leading peace negotiations with Russia, as the continent grapples with how to resolve the ongoing conflict in Ukraine.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚓⚔️ UK Royal Navy's entire submarine fleet out of service for maintenance
item.description: Britain's entire fleet of Astute-class submarines are currently out of service waiting for maintenance, raising concerns about the UK's ability to deter Russian stealth submarines and protect its Vanguard fleet.

item.title: 🇬🇧🏛️ Cabinet bodyguards working without security clearance
item.description: Bodyguards protecting senior UK Cabinet ministers have been operating without proper security clearance, according to a leaked letter raising serious concerns about ministerial safety.

item.title: ✈️📉 Global airlines slash 2026 profit forecast on fuel shock from Iran war
item.description: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) downgraded its 2026 profit outlook for the global airline industry, citing fuel price volatility and geopolitical disruption from the Iran conflict. Profits are expected to halve despite resilient passenger demand.

item.title: ⚽🇮🇷 Iran national soccer team arrives in Mexico for World Cup training
item.description: Iran squad lands in Mexico ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where they will prepare before group matches in the United States. The team faces US, England, and Ukraine in the group stage.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump rules out unfreezing Iranian assets before peace deal
item.description: In an NBC News interview, US President Donald Trump stated he would not unfreeze Iranian assets or lift sanctions until a comprehensive peace agreement is reached. The stance could complicate ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇮🇱 One killed, five wounded in central Israel terror shooting spree
item.description: A terrorist attack in communities bordering the West Bank north of Tel Aviv left one person dead and five wounded, with two in serious condition. One perpetrator was shot during the rampage.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Bridge linking Crimea to southern Ukraine damaged in drone strike
item.description: A drone strike damaged a bridge near the village of Chonhar connecting occupied Crimea with Russian-controlled parts of southern Ukraine, impacting a key logistics route for Russian forces.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 Trump-Xi summit on Taiwan offered more than optics
item.description: The meeting between Chinese and US presidents laid the groundwork for more predictable China-US relations, including on the Taiwan issue, beyond just symbolic photo opportunities.

item.title: 🇦🇲🗳️ Armenians Vote in Pivotal Election Testing Westward Shift Amid Russian Pressure
item.description: Armenia holds parliamentary elections seen as a test of PM Pashinyan's pivot away from Russia, with six pro-Russian opposition candidates arrested the day before the vote.
